prerequisites: Physical console access to the device (serial or USB console cable)
Ability to power-cycle the device (for ROMMON-based recovery)
Know the device platform and OS to select the correct procedure
Authorization to perform password recovery (security policy may require approval)
procedure_name: Password Recovery and AAA Lockout Recovery (iosxe)
purpose: Recover access to a device when admin credentials are lost or AAA server is unreachable [iosxe — method: ROMMON confreg 0x2142 (bypass startup-config); platforms: Catalyst 9000, Catalyst 8000, ASR 1000, ISR 4000]

rollback.action: No rollback needed — password reset is additive
If config was accidentally overwritten: restore from backup server

steps.action: Connect console cable and power-cycle the device
steps.notes: You must be on the console port. Terminal settings: 9600/8/N/1.
Send break signal during boot (Ctrl+Break on Windows, Ctrl+A then 'send break' on macOS/screen). Timing: within first 60 seconds of boot.

steps.action: Change config register to skip startup-config
steps.notes: 0x2142 tells the device to ignore startup-config on boot. The config is NOT deleted, just skipped.
Device boots with no configuration (factory default). All interfaces will be shutdown.
CRITICAL: Do NOT use 'copy running-config startup-config' — that would overwrite your saved config with the blank one. Load the saved config INTO running.

commands.command:
configure terminal
enable secret <new_password>
username admin privilege 15 secret <new_password>

steps.notes: Reset enable password and any local user passwords.
CRITICAL: If you forget this, the device will skip startup-config on EVERY reboot — effectively losing config on every reload.
Save the new password and restored config. Verify: show version | include Configuration register (should show 0x2102).

verification.confirms_if: Config register is back to normal — device will boot with config on next reload

verification.look_for: enable secret set to new value

verification.confirms_if: New password is saved to startup-config

warnings: Password recovery requires PHYSICAL console access — there is no remote recovery method
Some organizations disable password recovery for security (NX-OS: 'system no password-recovery', IOS-XE: 'no service password-recovery')
If password recovery is disabled, the ONLY option is to erase the configuration and start fresh
On IOS-XE: confreg 0x2142 skips startup-config on boot — do NOT forget to set it back to 0x2102
Document new credentials securely after recovery
If 'no service password-recovery' is configured, breaking into ROMMON triggers a full config erase
On stacked switches: password recovery on the Active switch recovers the entire stack
Catalyst 9000 with secure boot: ROMMON access may require additional authentication
NEVER forget step 7 (config-register 0x2102) — this is the most common mistake
